title: Make a business declaration for vehicle ownership
description: Companies, partnerships and sole traders must declare who controls a business-owned vehicle.
summary: Section 2 of the Road Traffic (Amendment) Act, 2018-31 says that any business that owns a vehicle must declare who is behind that business. The declaration tells the Barbados Licensing Authority who the owners, partners or directors are.
There is one version of the form for a sole trader, one for a partnership and one for a company. They all ask for the same things, just in slightly different layouts.

Make the declaration when you first register a business-owned vehicle, and update it whenever the ownership, partners or directors change. The Licensing Authority keeps the declaration on file alongside the vehicle’s registration.
You will need to bring your certificate of incorporation or business registration, photo ID for every signatory, and your business stamp or seal.
who_can_apply: a sole trader who owns one or more vehicles in the name of the business
a partnership where one or more partners own a vehicle
a company that owns one or more vehicles

items: the legal name of the business
the names, addresses and ID numbers of every owner, partner or director
the registration numbers of every vehicle covered

items.name: Certificate of incorporation or business registration

how_long: The declaration is recorded the same day.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Declaration Form (sole trader, partnership or company version) to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: We have merged the sole trader, partnership and company versions of the declaration onto one entry page.
‘Recorded the same day’ and ‘there is no fee’ are inferred from the form not stating either.
